20: describe  FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer  do
21:   subject { FileWatch::BufferedTokenizer.new }
22: 
23:   it "should tokenize a single token" do
24:     expect(subject.extract("foo\n")).to eq(["foo"])
25:   end
26: 
27:   it "should merge multiple token" do
28:     expect(subject.extract("foo")).to eq([])
29:     expect(subject.extract("bar\n")).to eq(["foobar"])
30:   end
31: 
32:   it "should tokenize multiple token" do
33:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar\n")).to eq(["foo", "bar"])
34:   end
35: 
36:   it "should ignore empty payload" do
37:     expect(subject.extract("")).to eq([])
38:     expect(subject.extract("foo\nbar")).to eq(["foo"])
39:   end
40: 
41:   it "should tokenize empty payload with newline" do
42:     expect(subject.extract("\n")).to eq([""])
43:     expect(subject.extract("\n\n\n")).to eq(["", "", ""])
44:   end
45: 
46:   describe 'flush' do

58:       expect(subject).to be_empty

105:     /**
106:      * Flush the contents of the input buffer, i.e. return the input buffer even though
107:      * a token has not yet been encountered
108:      *
109:      * @param context ThreadContext
110:      * @return Buffer contents
111:      */
112:     @JRubyMethod
113:     public IRubyObject flush(final ThreadContext context) {
114:         final IRubyObject buffer = joinInput(context);
115:         input.clear();
116:         inputSize = 0;
117:         return buffer;
118:     }
119: 
120:     @SuppressWarnings("rawtypes")
121:     private IRubyObject joinInput(final ThreadContext context) {
122:         final RubyArray parts = RubyUtil.RUBY.newArray(input.size());
123:         for (final IRubyObject part : input) {
124:             parts.append(part);
125:         }
126:         return parts.join(context);
127:     }
128: 
129:     @JRubyMethod(name = "empty?")
130:     public IRubyObject isEmpty(final ThreadContext context) {
131:         return RubyUtil.RUBY.newBoolean(input.isEmpty() && (inputSize == 0));
132:     }
133: 
134: }
